title: Today's AI Brief: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
summary: Pope Leo XIV's new encyclical on AI, Magnifica Humanitas, and Google I/O 2026 Dialogues stage highlights
imageAlt: Image from source article for How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageCaption: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageSourceTitle: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. The Pope has weighed in on AI with a new encyclical called Magnifica Humanitas, and Google just wrapped up their I/O conference. Let's dive in.

First up, Pope Leo XIV's Magnifica Humanitas is making waves. The encyclical includes a statement that's got technologists and policymakers talking: 'Technology is never neutral.' This is a big deal because it acknowledges that AI systems can reflect and amplify our values, or lack thereof. It's a call to think carefully about how we're building and using these technologies.

Next, I caught up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026. The conference brought together some big names to discuss the latest AI breakthroughs and their impact on society. One highlight was a discussion on the future of AI and its potential to drive positive change. It's always interesting to see what Google is working on, and this year's event did not disappoint.

Also on the board was a closer look at the economics of AI models. With the increasing power of these models comes a hefty price tag. Data boundaries, safety, trust, and distribution are all critical factors in the adoption of AI. It's a complex landscape, but one that's worth exploring.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
